findings.evidence: `rg -n "ofFinset|ofFinsetWithMultiplicity|indicator_nat|indicator_one" code/TauCeti mathlib/Mathlib -g '*.lean'` finds only `ofFinsuppMultiplicity` at line 74 and public `indicator_nat`/`indicator_one` lemmas starting at lines 175 and 306; no `ofFinsetWithMultiplicity` or `ofFinset` declaration exists, while roadmap lines 115 and 130 name symmetric powers/effective divisors and Abel maps as downstream targets.
findings.fix: Add public `def ofFinsetWithMultiplicity (s : Finset X) (m : X → ℕ)` and `def ofFinset (s : Finset X)` wrappers, move or de-emphasize raw `indicator_*` implementation lemmas, and expose the coefficient/support/degree/weighted-degree/pushforward/effectivity/degree-zero characteristic lemmas under the constructor names.
findings.issue: The public finite-set API is a family of lemmas about `(Finsupp.indicator s ... : WeilDivisor X)` rather than named constructors such as `ofFinsetWithMultiplicity` and `ofFinset`, exposing an implementation detail and forcing downstream Abel/symmetric-power code to spell the raw dependent `indicator` term.

summary: The finite-set divisor surface is exposed through raw `Finsupp.indicator` expressions instead of stable Weil-divisor constructors, so the API does not provide the named downstream object it is meant to support.
